perturbation.original_text: "Clinical Grade Product" means any Miltenyi Product designated as "Clinical Grade" in the attached Exhibit B, Column "Quality Status".
perturbation.changed_text: "Clinical Grade Product" means any Miltenyi Product designated as "Research Grade" in the attached Exhibit B, Column "Quality Status".
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Clinical Grade Product' is redefined to mean a 'Research Grade' product instead, creating a clear contradiction. This is because, a product cannot be both Clinical Grade and Research grade at the same time. This change introduces confusion as to what requirements apply to products designated as 'Clinical Grade' throughout the rest of the document. For example, Section 3.1(e) specifies that the Parties shall enter into a Quality Agreement relating to Clinical Grade Products. However, if Clinical Grade Products are now considered Research Grade Products, the necessity and scope of the Quality Agreement become uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Product Quality. (a) Product Specifications. Miltenyi shall manufacture or have manufactured the Miltenyi Products to meet the agreed Product Specifications, as then in effect, as published by Miltenyi from time to time, or as set forth in the Quality Agreement, as applicable. (b) Agreed Standards. All Miltenyi Products shall be manufactured and quality controlled in compliance with and pursuant to: (i) the Agreed Standards, (ii) the requirements of the Quality Agreement, if applicable, and (iii) Applicable Laws. (c) Testing. Miltenyi shall have standard analytical testing performed on each batch of Miltenyi Product to be shipped to Bellicum, in accordance with Agreed Standards and the procedures described in the corresponding documentation, to verify that Miltenyi Product meets Product Specifications and that it was manufactured in accordance with Agreed Standards and Applicable Laws. (d) Quality System. All Miltenyi Products supplied under this Agreement shall be manufactured and quality controlled under an appropriate quality system in accordance with Agreed Standards, as more fully described in the Quality Agreement (as applicable). Any subsequent change to Miltenyi's quality system that, as Bellicum can reasonably establish, would have or is likely to have a material effect on the safety, efficacy, identity and/or quality of a Miltenyi Product or its Permitted Use, requires the Parties to discuss and agree upon each such change in writing. (e) Quality Agreement. Within [...***...] days from the Effective Date (or such longer period as agreed by the Parties in writing, but in any event prior to the first delivery of Clinical Grade Product to Bellicum), the Parties shall enter into an agreement on mutually acceptable, commercially reasonable terms that details the quality assurance obligations of each Party relating to Clinical Grade Products (the "Quality Agreement"). In the event of a conflict between the terms of the Quality Agreement and the terms of this Agreement, the provisions of this Agreement shall govern; provided, however, that the Quality Agreement shall govern in respect of quality issues.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Clinical Grade Product" to mean "Research Grade" product, directly contradicting the original intention of the contract. Section 3.1(e) explicitly requires a Quality Agreement for "Clinical Grade Products". By redefining Clinical Grade Products as Research Grade Products, the changed definition casts serious doubt on whether the Quality Agreement, and the quality control measures described in Section 3.1, apply. This ambiguity creates a direct and significant contradiction, making it unclear which standards and obligations apply to products previously understood as 'Clinical Grade'.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement shall have an initial term of ten (10) years commencing from the Effective Date and ending on the tenth (10th) anniversary thereof (the "Initial Term"), unless earlier terminated by either Party in accordance with the provisions of Section 15.2 or Section 15.3.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement shall have an initial term of one (1) year commencing from the Effective Date and ending on the one (1st) anniversary thereof (the "Initial Term"), unless earlier terminated by either Party in accordance with the provisions of Section 15.2 or Section 15.3.
perturbation.explanation: Here, the Initial Term of the agreement is changed from ten (10) years to one (1) year. This creates a contradiction because financial obligations, forecasts, and strategic plans are likely built around a longer timeframe. Changing the initial term impacts several sections that rely on that term for duration calculations and expectations. This also directly contradicts Section 15.1 regarding renewal terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Forecasts. In order to assist Miltenyi with its capacity, procurement and production planning, and as a general framework for forecasting Bellicum's orders of Miltenyi Products (where more specific parameters may be set forth in a given Module), Bellicum agrees to provide Miltenyi with rolling forecasts of Bellicum's (and its Subcontractors' and Licensees') anticipated quantity requirements for Miltenyi Products in the Forecast Territory during the Term of this Agreement, in accordance with the provisions of this Section 5.1 (each, a "Forecast"). There is no binding forecasting obligation for Research Grade Products, except (if applicable) as otherwise explicitly agreed in a Module. Any modified forecasting terms and conditions for a particular Bellicum Product or Bellicum Program that supplement this Article 5 will be set forth in the Module applicable to that Bellicum Product or Bellicum Program. All of the Forecasts provided under this Agreement will break down the demand of Miltenyi Products on a product-by-product (expressed in number of units) and manufacturing country-by-manufacturing country basis (i.e., Forecast Territory only) and substantially follow the mutually agreed Miltenyi forecast sheet, as attached hereto in Exhibit C 1-3. All Forecasts provided by Bellicum will be good faith estimates of Bellicum's anticipated quantity requirements for Miltenyi Products during the relevant period. Bellicum agrees to use [...***...] in preparing all Forecasts provided hereunder to minimize variances between Forecasts. Each Forecast shall be duly signed by an authorized representative of Bellicum (or Bellicum's designee on behalf of Bellicum) and submitted in writing to Miltenyi, by mail, email or facsimile, and shall supersede prior Forecasts to the extent the Forecast overlaps with prior Forecasts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the "Initial Term" from ten (10) years to one (1) year. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 5.1, which discusses forecasts "during the Term of this Agreement". Since the forecasts are critical for capacity, procurement, and production planning, the change from a 10-year term to a 1-year term will significantly impact and contradict the feasibility and relevance of the forecasts described in Section 5.1. The contradicted section refers to forecasts covering the entire term, which is rendered nonsensical if the term is suddenly reduced, thus creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: To Miltenyi's knowledge and after due inquiry, on the Effective Date, Miltenyi owns all right, title, and interest in and to, or otherwise possesses all necessary rights and licenses under, the Miltenyi Technology and the Miltenyi Intellectual Property Rights, to perform its obligations under this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: To Bellicum's knowledge and after due inquiry, on the Effective Date, Bellicum owns all right, title, and interest in and to, or otherwise possesses all necessary rights and licenses under, the Miltenyi Technology and the Miltenyi Intellectual Property Rights, to perform its obligations under this Agreement
perturbation.explanation: The ownership of Miltenyi Technology and Intellectual Property Rights is changed from Miltenyi to Bellicum. This creates a direct in-text contradiction within the article. The agreement is based on Miltenyi providing the product to Bellicum, not the other way around. This undermines Miltenyi's warranty and its rights to the product being supplied. The main purpose of this agreement becomes unfulfillable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1 Existing Intellectual Property. Except as the Parties may otherwise expressly agree in writing, each Party shall continue to own all rights, including all Intellectual Property Rights, in and title to its Technology existing as of the Effective Date or developed during the Term but outside the scope of this Agreement, without conferring any interests therein on the other Party. Without limiting the generality of the preceding sentence, as between the Parties, the Parties acknowledge and agree that (i) Miltenyi owns and shall continue to own all rights (including all Intellectual Property Rights) in the Miltenyi Technology included in the Miltenyi Products supplied to Bellicum, and Bellicum shall not acquire any right, interest in or title to the Miltenyi Technology by virtue of this Agreement or otherwise, and (ii) Bellicum owns or controls and shall continue to own and control all rights (including all Intellectual Property Rights) in the Bellicum Technology and Bellicum Products (and any Intellectual Property rights thereof), and Miltenyi shall not acquire any right, interest in or title to the Bellicum Technology and Bellicum Products (and any Intellectual Property rights thereof) by virtue of this Agreement or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Bellicum owns the Miltenyi Technology and Intellectual Property Rights, which directly contradicts section 10.1 which states that Miltenyi owns the Miltenyi Technology included in the Miltenyi Products supplied to Bellicum, and Bellicum shall not acquire any right, interest in or title to the Miltenyi Technology by virtue of this Agreement or otherwise.
